operation: First create an XZ-aligned workplane, sketch a closed rectangular profile with an X-axis span of 3 units (from x=5 to x=8) and Z-axis span of 4 units (from z=0 to z=4), then extrude this profile 2 units along the Y axis to form a base rectangular prism. Next, select the maximum Y-facing face of the prism, establish a new workplane on this face, draw a regular 5-sided pentagon with a circumradius of 4 units, mirror this pentagon across the workplane's Y axis to produce a second identical pentagon, and finally execute a 1.5 unit deep blind cut into the prism using the two pentagonal profiles as the cutting geometry.
geometry: The model is a rectangular cuboid base block with two identical regular pentagonal recessed cutouts positioned symmetrically across the vertical centerline of one of its larger flat faces. The pentagonal cutouts extend through most of the thickness of the cuboid, leaving a thin, uniform solid wall on the opposite face of the block.
function: This is a symmetric mounting block with paired matching pentagonal sockets, designed to securely align and hold two pentagonal mating components or fasteners in a fixed parallel arrangement, with its solid rear wall providing structural support for loaded assemblies.
